# ollog — Full LLM Reference

A self-hosted, ADIF-native, multi-operator ham radio logbook. This document is the
machine-readable full reference for LLM tooling. Content covers the REST API, ADIF
field conventions, and an operator walkthrough.

Multiple operators log QSOs simultaneously under their own callsigns without conflicts.
Data is stored verbatim in ADIF field format. Hosted on a single server; accessed via
browser or REST API from any location.

## Table of Contents

- API Reference:         /llms-full.txt#api-reference
- ADIF Field Reference:  /llms-full.txt#adif-field-reference
- Getting Started:       /llms-full.txt#getting-started

---

## API Reference

Base URL (default): http://localhost:8000

All request and response bodies are JSON unless otherwise specified.
Timestamps returned by the API are UTC ISO-8601 strings.

### Authentication

ollog supports three authentication mechanisms:

1. Bearer JWT (primary)
   Obtain a token via POST /auth/token. Include in all subsequent requests:
     Authorization: Bearer <token>
   Default expiry: 480 minutes (8 hours — suitable for overnight FT8 sessions).

2. X-API-Key header (alternative for QSO endpoints)
   Create a named API key via POST /api/tokens/. Include in requests:
     X-API-Key: <token>
   API keys work only on QSO endpoints (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/qsos/).
   They are NOT accepted on auth, profile, tokens, adif, or admin endpoints.

3. HttpOnly cookie (browser/UI)
   The web UI uses a session cookie set on login. Cookie auth is transparent
   to browser users. REST API callers should use Bearer JWT or X-API-Key.

Operator isolation: every QSO is scoped to the authenticated operator's callsign
(from the JWT). Operators cannot read, modify, or delete each other's QSOs.

---

### POST /auth/token

Auth: None
Description: OAuth2-compatible login endpoint. Authenticates with username and password;
             returns a signed JWT access token valid for 480 minutes by default.
Request: Form-encoded body (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)
  username  (string, required) — operator username
  password  (string, required) — operator password
Response: JSON
  {
    "access_token": "<jwt>",
    "token_type": "bearer"
  }
Status codes:
  200 OK           — credentials valid; token returned
  401 Unauthorized — bad username or password

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/token \
    -d "username=myuser&password=mypass"

---

### GET /auth/me

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Returns the authenticated operator's username, callsign, and role.
Request: none
Response: JSON
  {
    "username": "myuser",
    "callsign": "W1AW",
    "role": "operator"
  }
Status codes:
  200 OK           — user info returned
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid token

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8000/auth/me

---

### POST /api/qsos/

Auth: Bearer JWT or X-API-Key
Description: Create a new QSO for the authenticated operator. OPERATOR and
             STATION_CALLSIGN are auto-stamped from the authenticated user's profile.
             BAND and MODE are uppercased on ingest. Duplicate detection window: ±2 minutes
             same CALL+BAND+MODE. Set ?force=true to override duplicate detection.
Request: JSON body
  {
    "CALL":     "W1AW",       (string, required) callsign of the contacted station
    "QSO_DATE": "20240115",   (string, required) UTC date in YYYYMMDD format
    "TIME_ON":  "1430",       (string, required) UTC start time in HHMM or HHMMSS format
    "BAND":     "20m",        (string, required) amateur band designator (e.g. 40m, 20m, 2m)
    "MODE":     "SSB",        (string, required) operating mode (e.g. SSB, CW, FT8, FM)
    "FREQ":     "14.225",     (string, optional) frequency in MHz
    "RST_SENT": "59",         (string, optional) RST signal report sent
    "RST_RCVD": "59",         (string, optional) RST signal report received
    ...                       any additional ADIF fields accepted and stored verbatim
  }
Response: JSON — created QSO record including server-assigned "id"
Status codes:
  201 Created  — QSO logged successfully
  409 Conflict — duplicate QSO detected (add ?force=true to override)
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid credentials
  422 Unprocessable Entity — validation error (missing required field)

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/ \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"CALL":"W1AW","QSO_DATE":"20240115","TIME_ON":"1430","BAND":"20m","MODE":"SSB"}'

---

### GET /api/qsos/

Auth: Bearer JWT or X-API-Key
Description: List the authenticated operator's active QSOs with pagination and optional
             filters. Returns only non-deleted QSOs owned by the caller.
Request: Query parameters (all optional)
  page       (int, default 1)    — page number (1-based)
  page_size  (int, default 50)   — results per page (max 500)
  call       (string)            — filter by contacted callsign (partial match)
  band       (string)            — filter by band (e.g. 20m)
  mode       (string)            — filter by mode (e.g. FT8)
  date_from  (string, YYYYMMDD)  — filter QSOs on or after this UTC date
  date_to    (string, YYYYMMDD)  — filter QSOs on or before this UTC date
  sort       (string, default "-qso_date_utc") — sort field with optional "-" prefix for desc
Response: JSON
  {
    "items":     [ ...QSOResponse objects... ],
    "total":     142,
    "page":      1,
    "page_size": 50
  }
Status codes:
  200 OK           — list returned (empty list if no matching QSOs)
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid credentials

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    "http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/?band=20m&mode=FT8&page=1&page_size=25"

---

### GET /api/qsos/{qso_id}

Auth: Bearer JWT or X-API-Key
Description: Fetch a single QSO by its MongoDB ObjectId. Returns 404 if the QSO does not
             exist, is not owned by the caller, or has been soft-deleted.
Request: Path parameter
  qso_id  (string) — MongoDB ObjectId of the QSO
Response: JSON — single QSO record
Status codes:
  200 OK           — QSO found and returned
  404 Not Found    — QSO not found, not owned, or deleted
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid credentials

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    "http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/$QSO_ID"

---

### PATCH /api/qsos/{qso_id}

Auth: Bearer JWT or X-API-Key
Description: Partially update a QSO. Only fields present in the request body are updated.
             Protected fields (_operator, _deleted, _id) are stripped. BAND and MODE are
             uppercased if present. qso_date_utc is recalculated when QSO_DATE or TIME_ON
             changes.
Request: Path parameter + JSON body
  qso_id  (string) — MongoDB ObjectId of the QSO
  Body: any ADIF fields to update, e.g.
    {
      "RST_SENT": "59",
      "COMMENT":  "Great signal from EU"
    }
Response: JSON — updated QSO record
Status codes:
  200 OK           — update applied; updated record returned
  404 Not Found    — QSO not found or not owned by caller
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid credentials

curl example:
  curl -X PATCH http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/$QSO_ID \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"RST_SENT":"59","COMMENT":"Strong signal"}'

---

### DELETE /api/qsos/{qso_id}

Auth: Bearer JWT or X-API-Key
Description: Soft-delete a QSO. The record is marked _deleted=true in MongoDB — it is NOT
             physically removed. Soft-deleted QSOs are excluded from list, get, and export.
             To permanently remove data, direct database access is required.
Request: Path parameter
  qso_id  (string) — MongoDB ObjectId of the QSO
Response: none (empty body)
Status codes:
  204 No Content — soft-delete applied
  404 Not Found  — QSO not found, not owned, or already deleted
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid credentials

curl example:
  curl -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    "http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/$QSO_ID"

---

### POST /api/adif/import

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Import an ADIF (.adi or .adif) file and return a JSON import report.
             Accepted per-record: one QSO inserted on success. Duplicate detection (±2 min
             window) and per-record error accumulation — no silent drops. File size limit: 10 MB.
Request: Multipart form data
  file  (UploadFile, required) — the .adi/.adif file to import
Response: JSON — import report
  {
    "total_records": 5,
    "accepted":    [ {"record_index": 0, "call": "W1AW", "id": "<mongo_id>"}, ... ],
    "duplicates":  [ {"record_index": 2, "call": "K1TTT", "existing_id": "<id>"}, ... ],
    "errors":      [ {"record_index": 4, "call": null, "error": "Missing CALL field"}, ... ]
  }
Status codes:
  200 OK                    — import processed; report returned (check per-record status)
  413 Request Entity Too Large — file exceeds 10 MB
  401 Unauthorized          — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/adif/import \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -F "file=@logbook.adi"

---

### GET /api/adif/export

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Stream the authenticated operator's full logbook as a valid .adi ADIF file
             download. Filters: only the caller's non-deleted QSOs. Output is a standard
             ADIF 3.1.4 file with ADIF_VER, PROGRAMID headers, and one record per QSO.
Request: none
Response: text/plain streaming download (Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=<callsign>_logbook.adi)
Status codes:
  200 OK           — ADIF stream starts immediately (StreamingResponse)
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    http://localhost:8000/api/adif/export -o logbook.adi

---

### GET /api/profile/

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Return the authenticated operator's profile fields. A user with no profile
             fields set receives all-null optional fields.
Request: none
Response: JSON — profile object
  {
    "username":          "myuser",
    "callsign":          "W1AW",
    "station_callsign":  "W1AW/1",
    "gridsquare":        "FN42",
    "lat":               42.5,
    "lon":               -71.5,
    "rig":               "Icom IC-7300",
    "antenna":           "Dipole"
  }
Status codes:
  200 OK           — profile returned
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8000/api/profile/

---

### PATCH /api/profile/

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Partially update the authenticated operator's profile. Only fields present in
             the request body are updated. When gridsquare is updated, latitude and longitude
             are auto-computed from the grid center.
Request: JSON body — any subset of profile fields:
  {
    "callsign":         "W1AW",
    "station_callsign": "W1AW/1",
    "gridsquare":       "FN42",
    "rig":              "Icom IC-7300",
    "antenna":          "Dipole"
  }
Response: JSON — updated profile object
Status codes:
  200 OK           — update applied; updated profile returned
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -X PATCH http://localhost:8000/api/profile/ \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"callsign":"W1AW","station_callsign":"W1AW/1","gridsquare":"FN42"}'

---

### POST /api/tokens/

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Create a named API token for the authenticated user. Returns the full plaintext
             token exactly once in the response body. The raw token is never stored — only
             the HMAC-SHA256 hash is persisted. Use the token with X-API-Key header on QSO
             endpoints.
Request: Form-encoded body (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)
  name        (string, required)          — human-readable token name
  expires_at  (string, optional YYYY-MM-DD) — expiry date (omit for no expiry)
Response: JSON — includes full_token (present only in this response; not retrievable again)
  {
    "id":           "<token_id>",
    "name":         "my-logging-script",
    "token_prefix": "tok_",
    "created_at":   "2024-01-15T14:30:00Z",
    "expires_at":   null,
    "enabled":      true,
    "full_token":   "tok_<plaintext>"
  }
Status codes:
  201 Created      — token created; full_token in response
  422 Unprocessable Entity — invalid name or bad date format
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/tokens/ \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -d "name=my-script"

---

### GET /api/tokens/

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: List all active (non-revoked) API tokens for the authenticated user. Does not
             return the full plaintext token — only metadata (id, name, prefix, dates).
Request: none
Response: JSON array of token objects (same shape as POST response minus full_token)
Status codes:
  200 OK           — list returned (empty array if no active tokens)
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8000/api/tokens/

---

### DELETE /api/tokens/{token_id}

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Revoke (disable) an API token owned by the authenticated user. Sets enabled=false;
             the token can no longer authenticate requests. Returns 404 if not found or not
             owned by the caller.
Request: Path parameter
  token_id  (string) — token ID (from GET /api/tokens/ or POST /api/tokens/ response)
Response: none (empty body)
Status codes:
  204 No Content — token revoked
  404 Not Found  — token not found or not owned by caller
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    "http://localhost:8000/api/tokens/$TOKEN_ID"

---

### GET /health

Auth: None
Description: MongoDB health check. Returns 200 if the database connection is healthy,
             503 if MongoDB is unreachable.
Request: none
Response: JSON
  {"status": "ok"}    — MongoDB reachable
  {"status": "error", "detail": "<message>"}  — MongoDB unreachable
Status codes:
  200 OK                   — healthy
  503 Service Unavailable  — database unreachable

curl example:
  curl http://localhost:8000/health

---

### GET /api/whoami

Auth: Bearer JWT
Description: Returns the authenticated operator's callsign as extracted from the JWT
             payload. Lightweight identity check — no database query.
Request: none
Response: JSON
  {"callsign": "W1AW"}
Status codes:
  200 OK           — callsign returned
  401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid JWT

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8000/api/whoami

---

## ADIF Field Reference

ollog stores QSO data in ADIF (Amateur Data Interchange Format) field format. ADIF field
names are uppercase strings. Field values are stored verbatim (as strings) in MongoDB.
Any ADIF field can be included in a POST or PATCH request body and will be persisted.

### Required Fields

These five fields are required for every QSO. The server rejects records missing any of them.

  Field       Format           Example     Description
  ----------  ---------------  ----------  ------------------------------------------
  CALL        string           W1AW        Callsign of the contacted (logged) station
  QSO_DATE    YYYYMMDD         20240115    UTC date of the QSO
  TIME_ON     HHMM or HHMMSS   1430        UTC start time (HHMM, e.g. 1430 = 14:30)
  BAND        string           20m         Amateur band designator (e.g. 40m, 20m, 2m)
  MODE        string           SSB         Operating mode (SSB, CW, FT8, FM, RTTY, etc.)

QSO_DATE format: YYYYMMDD — eight digits, no separators. Example: 20240115 = 15 January 2024.
TIME_ON format:  HHMM (four digits) or HHMMSS (six digits). Example: 1430 = 14:30 UTC.

### Optional Fields

These fields are accepted and stored if present; absent fields are not required.

  Field        Format   Example       Description
  -----------  -------  ------------  -------------------------------------------
  FREQ         string   14.225        Frequency in MHz (decimal)
  RST_SENT     string   59            RST report sent to contacted station
  RST_RCVD     string   59            RST report received from contacted station
  TX_PWR       string   100           Transmit power in watts
  COMMENT      string   Nice signal   Free-text comment about the QSO
  QTH          string   Boston MA     Contacted station's location
  GRIDSQUARE   string   FN42          Contacted station's Maidenhead grid locator
  CONTEST_ID   string   CQWW-SSB      Contest identifier per ADIF spec
  SRX          string   042           Serial number received in contest exchange
  STX          string   001           Serial number sent in contest exchange

### Auto-Stamped Fields

These fields are automatically populated by the server from the authenticated operator's
JWT and profile. Do NOT include them in request bodies — they will be ignored or overwritten.

  Field             Source           Description
  ----------------  ---------------  -------------------------------------------------
  OPERATOR          JWT callsign     Callsign of the person operating (logging) the QSO
  STATION_CALLSIGN  Operator profile Licensed callsign of the station equipment being used

OPERATOR identifies the operator at the keyboard; STATION_CALLSIGN identifies the
station's licensed callsign (may differ during guest operations or /P activations).

### Application-Specific Fields

  Field             Used in       Description
  ----------------  ------------  ---------------------------------------------------
  APP_OLLOG_TOKEN   UDP datagrams API token for multi-operator UDP routing. Include
                                  in ADIF datagrams sent from logging software (e.g.
                                  WSJT-X) to route the QSO to the correct operator.

### Conventions

- BAND designators are strings: 160m, 80m, 60m, 40m, 30m, 20m, 17m, 15m, 12m, 10m,
  6m, 4m, 2m, 70cm, etc. BAND values are uppercased on ingest.
- MODE is uppercased on ingest: SSB, CW, FT8, FT4, FM, AM, RTTY, PSK31, etc.
- All timestamps are UTC. QSO_DATE and TIME_ON are stored as strings; qso_date_utc is
  a computed internal datetime field used for duplicate detection and sorting.
- Duplicate detection: ±2 minutes same CALL+BAND+MODE per operator. The 2-minute window
  handles clock skew in automated logging software. Override with ?force=true.
- Extra ADIF fields beyond the declared set are accepted via extra="allow" on the
  QSOCreateRequest model and stored verbatim in MongoDB.

---

## Getting Started

This walkthrough covers the six steps to go from a fresh installation to a working
multi-operator logbook: login, set profile, log a QSO via the web UI, log a QSO via
the REST API, export your logbook, and import an ADIF file.

### 1. Login

Authenticate with your username and password to obtain a JWT access token. Save the
token — you will use it in subsequent steps.

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/token \
    -d "username=myuser&password=mypass"

Response:
  {
    "access_token": "<your-jwt-token>",
    "token_type": "bearer"
  }

Export the token for use in subsequent commands:
  export TOKEN="<your-jwt-token>"

Default token lifetime: 480 minutes (8 hours). Re-authenticate when it expires.

### 2. Set up your profile

Before logging QSOs, set your callsign and optional profile fields. The callsign is
stamped on every QSO you log as OPERATOR. STATION_CALLSIGN identifies the station.

curl example:
  curl -X PATCH http://localhost:8000/api/profile/ \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"callsign":"W1AW","station_callsign":"W1AW","gridsquare":"FN42"}'

Verify your profile was saved:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8000/api/profile/

### 3. Log a QSO via the web UI

Open a browser and navigate to:
  http://localhost:8000/log/

Fill in the entry form with the required fields: CALL, BAND, MODE, and TIME_ON. Submit
the form. The new QSO appears in the live log table instantly via Server-Sent Events.

The web UI is the fastest way to log while operating — no curl required.

### 4. Log a QSO via the REST API

POST a QSO with the five required ADIF fields. Additional ADIF fields are optional and
stored verbatim.

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/ \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "CALL":     "W1AW",
      "QSO_DATE": "20240115",
      "TIME_ON":  "1430",
      "BAND":     "20m",
      "MODE":     "SSB",
      "RST_SENT": "59",
      "RST_RCVD": "59"
    }'

On success, the response is 201 Created with the saved QSO record including its "id".
If the QSO is a duplicate (same CALL+BAND+MODE within ±2 minutes), the server returns
409 Conflict. Add ?force=true to override.

Retrieve the logged QSO list:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8000/api/qsos/

### 5. Export your logbook as ADIF

Download your full logbook as a standard ADIF (.adi) file:

curl example:
  curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    http://localhost:8000/api/adif/export -o logbook.adi

The export file contains all non-deleted QSOs for your callsign in ADIF 3.1.4 format,
with ADIF_VER and PROGRAMID headers. Import this file into any ADIF-compatible logging
software (e.g. Log4OM, TQSL, LOTW, DX4WIN).

### 6. Import an ADIF file

Import a .adi or .adif file from another logging program. Each record is validated
(required fields checked), duplicate-detected, and reported individually.

curl example:
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/adif/import \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -F "file=@logbook.adi"

The response is a JSON import report:
  {
    "total_records": 10,
    "accepted":   [ ... ],
    "duplicates": [ ... ],
    "errors":     [ ... ]
  }

Check the "errors" list for any records that failed validation. Duplicate records are
listed separately — they are not re-imported but are not counted as errors.

---

End of ollog Full LLM Reference.
